extra.message: User input from 'username' parameter is directly concatenated into SQL query string passed to run_sql_query(). This allows SQL injection attacks where an attacker can modify the query logic or extract unauthorized data.

User input from User model fields (name, username, password, address, email, contact) are directly concatenated into SQL INSERT query string. An attacker can inject SQL code through any of these fields to execute arbitrary SQL commands.
User input from 'username' parameter is directly concatenated into SQL DELETE query string. An attacker can inject SQL code to delete unauthorized data or modify query logic.

User input from 'user.address' field is directly concatenated into SQL DELETE query string without quotes and without parameterization. This allows SQL injection where an attacker can manipulate the query logic.

User input from request.json() is directly used in get_nosql_users() query parameter without validation. This allows NoSQL injection where an attacker can craft malicious JSON objects to bypass queries or extract unauthorized data (e.g., {"username": {"$ne": null}}).
Application title from app.title is directly concatenated into HTML response without escaping. If app.title contains user-controlled data or can be modified, this allows stored XSS attacks.
Application title from app.title is directly concatenated into HTML response without escaping. If app.title contains user-controlled data or can be modified, this allows stored XSS attacks.

User password (even though hashed) is stored in NoSQL database without additional encryption. The password field should be removed from storage or encrypted separately. Additionally, line 172 shows passwords are being popped from query results, but they are still stored.

cwe: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
